marketState: bullish tilt — extreme cooling demand and washed-out spec positioning offset by a bearish storage surplus
summary: Weather is the standout signal: both the 1-7D and 8-16D demand windows are running ~29-31% above the 5-year norm with rangePos above 1.0, i.e. forecast demand exceeds the entire historical range, and the trend confirms +30% vs 5y-normal. Seasonality is mildly constructive (price already +2.2pp above the typical year, 63% odds of being higher in 4 weeks, +2.3% typical 4-week move) but the 51pp 10-90% spread makes it a low-reliability signal. Front price ($3.205) sits inside the fair-value band ($2.429-$3.429, fair $2.929) at 'SLIGHTLY OVER' now, but that premium shrinks to 'FAIR' by +14D/+21D as the fair-value band itself rises to meet price — the market is not badly stretched. Managed money is net short at only the 18th percentile of the 5-year range (contrarian bullish, fuel for short covering into heat), while storage is a genuine headwind at +175 Bcf vs 5y with a bigger-than-forecast build (bearish surprise), and daily/weekly technicals show a bearish trend structure (50<200 EMA, negative RSI momentum). Net read: near-term heat-driven demand and stretched short positioning argue for upside risk that storage bears and daily-chart trend followers are underpricing, but the surplus caps how far a rally can run without a supportive storage print.

longTerm.entry: $3.05-$3.20 (scale in on dips toward fair value)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk (risk ≈ $0.20-0.35/MMBtu per contract), scale in over 2 tranches
longTerm.rationale: Both weather windows show cooling demand running 29-31% above 5-year norms and above the historical max range, the strongest single input in this dataset, and typically forces storage builds to shrink over the following weeks against the current +175 Bcf surplus. Seasonality (63% odds higher in 4 weeks, Cooling season, price already running above the typical-year path) and a contrarian-bullish COT setup (managed money net short at just the 18th percentile of 5 years) reinforce the case for upside over a multi-week horizon.
longTerm.invalidation: A cooldown in the 8-16D demand forecast back toward/below the 5y norm, or a string of larger-than-forecast storage builds that push the deviation further above +175 Bcf vs 5y, would invalidate this view.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term demand (1-7D) is the most extreme reading in the dataset at +30.6% vs 5y and above the max range, and the front month trades at a slight premium to Sep/Oct (curve backwardation into the front), consistent with immediate physical tightness. This offsets mixed-to-bearish intraday/daily technicals — bearish daily and weekly trend, but 5m trend bullish with RSI near neutral, leaving room to reprice higher on continued heat.
shortTerm.invalidation: A break and close back below the daily EMA structure with RSI rolling over below 40, or a sharp drop in the 1-7D demand forecast, would flip this to neutral/short.

keyDrivers: 1-7D and 8-16D cooling demand both ~29-31% above 5-year norms and above the historical max range
Managed-money net short at just the 18th percentile of 5 years — contrarian bullish fuel for short covering
Front price sits inside the fair-value band, with the band itself rising to meet price over the next 3 weeks
Storage +175 Bcf vs 5-year average with a bigger-than-forecast build (bearish surprise)
Daily/weekly technicals show a bearish trend (50<200 EMA), conflicting with the weather/COT bullish case
risks: Seasonality reliability is Low (51pp 10-90% spread) — the probHigher/typical-move stats carry wide uncertainty
Storage surplus is a real overhang; if builds stay elevated even through hot weather, price could revert to/through fair value
Daily/weekly technical trend is bearish, so a heat-driven rally could still be a countertrend move that fails at resistance
